title: UX: Fix command has no --dry-run mode
description: The fix command writes files immediately with no preview. Users cannot see what would change before committing. Should add --dry-run flag that shows diffs without writing.

title: Rename three visit() functions in analyzer.ts
description: analyzer.ts has three functions named visit() at different scopes: (1) line 138 inside buildCallGraph, (2) line 169 inside extractFunctions, (3) line 192 at module level. The module-level visit is called by findNestedFunctionViolations at line 63 — 129 lines away. All three experts flagged this (3/3 consensus, 10/10 confidence). Rename to visitForCallGraph, visitForFunctionExtraction, visitForNestedViolations. The tool enforces naming clarity in user code but violates it in its own source.
close_reason: Renamed four visit() functions to descriptive names

title: UX: --rules help text doesn't list available rule IDs
description: The --rules option says 'Comma-separated rule ids to run (default: all)' but doesn't tell users what IDs exist. Should say 'Available: stepdown, nested' or add a 'list-rules' subcommand.
close_reason: Already resolved: cli.ts line 28 already reads 'Comma-separated rule IDs (available: stepdown, nested; default: all)'

title: BUG: Summary line has duplicate 'in N files' text
description: In cli.ts:163, the summary says 'Found 19 violations and 1 circular dependency in 17 files in 17 files'. formatCircularSummary() already includes 'in N files', and then line 163 appends 'in ${totalFiles} files' again. When both violations AND circular deps exist, the text is duplicated.

title: UX: --verbose description is misleading
description: The verbose flag is described as 'Show circular dependencies in output' but users expect verbose to mean more detail on everything. Current behavior: only shows circular deps when violations=0, and shows 'No files needed fixing' in fix mode. Should either rename to --show-cycles or make verbose actually show richer output.
close_reason: Updated --verbose description to accurately reflect behavior

title: README documents nonexistent features (--fix, --output-file, config options)
description: README.md shows: (1) 'stepdown-rule --fix' but --fix is not a top-level flag, must use 'stepdown-rule fix' subcommand. (2) '--output-file' option that does not exist. (3) Config options (analyzeArrowFunctions, analyzeExportsOnly, reportCircularDependencies) that are not implemented — schema only supports 'ignore'. (4) Programmatic API passes config options that Config type doesn't support.

title: Fix cycle detection early-return missing cycles
description: analyzer.ts:130-153 — dfsDetectCycle returns true on first cycle found, causing DFS to short-circuit and miss additional cycles in the same connected component. Either remove the early return or replace with the graph-algorithms.ts version which doesn't have this issue.

title: Delete dead migration comments in fixer and analyzer
description: fixer.ts and analyzer.ts contain archaeological comments from past refactorings that describe where code used to live, not what current code does. Examples: '// CATEGORIZATION: Now uses unified ast-node-visitors module', '// NODE CLASSIFICATION: Moved to ast-node-visitors', '// callGraphToDependencyMap: Now uses unified ast-graph-builder module'. These become lies over time and accumulate sediment. Delete all '// Now uses...', '// Moved to...' style comments.
close_reason: Deleted 4 dead migration comments from analyzer.ts and fixer.ts

title: Add registry factory for test isolation
description: registry.ts uses a process-scoped mutable singleton (const rules = []) with no reset mechanism. Tests are order-dependent: registry.test.ts 'list returns empty' only passes if it runs before 'register and list'. vic-pipeline.test.ts imports register-default-rules as a side effect, polluting the global array for any subsequent test. Add createRegistry() factory that returns { register, getEnabled, list } closure. analyzeFiles and runPipeline accept optional registry param defaulting to module-level instance. Export reset() gated behind test-only guard as interim measure.

title: UX: Silent output when glob matches zero files
description: Running 'stepdown-rule analyze nonexistent/**/*.ts' produces zero output and exits 0. Users get no feedback that their pattern matched nothing. Should warn 'No files matched pattern: ...'.

title: Harden type guards, mutation, and position map
description: Three hardening items: (1) analyzer.ts:580 isStepdownViolation uses fragile 'dependency in v' check — use discriminated union with type field instead. (2) graph-algorithms.ts findAndRemoveLeafFunctions mutates its input Map — clone before mutating or document the contract. (3) fixer.ts:480-491 buildPositionMap uses trimmed line as key — identical function signatures collide, giving wrong countFunctionMovements results. Use line+index composite key.
notes: Expert review (3/3 consensus): (1) Add discriminant `kind: 'stepdown' | 'nested'` field to StepdownViolation and NestedFunctionViolation. Replace structural sniffing (`'dependency' in v`) with `v.kind === 'stepdown'`. Both inline and named type guards are inconsistent — one uses named guard, other uses anonymous `'nested' in v`. (2) findAndRemoveLeafFunctions should return `[leafNames, remainingDeps]` tuple instead of mutating input map. Current safety depends on defensive copy in caller (fixer.ts:229).

title: UX: Silent output on zero violations (no success confirmation)
description: When analyzing files with zero violations, the CLI produces absolutely no output. Users cannot tell if analysis ran, if files were found, or if patterns matched. Should print something like '5 files analyzed, 0 violations found'.

title: Make dependencyGraph required on AnalysisResult
description: types.ts:42 declares dependencyGraph?: Map<string, string[]> as optional, but every code path populates it: analyzeParsedFile (line 53), violationsToAnalysisResult (line 110), buildRuleContext (line 124). The field is optional in the type but mandatory in practice. This forces unnecessary null guards downstream and hides the actual invariant. Make it required. The fallback in reorderFunctionDeclarations (fixer.ts:228) is still useful for direct callers but AnalysisResult should reflect truth.

title: Remove phantom file field from violation interfaces
description: StepdownViolation.file and NestedFunctionViolation.file are always set to empty string at construction (analyzer.ts:419, 510). The real file path lives on AnalysisResult.file. The CLI reads result.file for display and ignores violation.file entirely. The field is a required non-optional string that is always empty and never read — worst of both worlds. Either remove file from both violation interfaces (AnalysisResult is the natural container) or populate it correctly by passing filePath through the call chain.

title: UX: No default command — bare 'stepdown-rule' shows help instead of analyzing
description: Running 'stepdown-rule' with no args shows help and exits 1. Most linter CLIs (eslint, biome) run analysis by default. Should make 'analyze' the default command so 'stepdown-rule' is equivalent to 'stepdown-rule analyze'.

title: Consolidate duplicate buildCallGraph and CallSiteInfo
description: Two issues: (1) buildCallGraph exists in both analyzer.ts:168-198 (with real positions) and ast-graph-builder.ts (with placeholder {line:0, column:0}). (2) CallSiteInfo interface is defined in both rule-context.ts and ast-graph-builder.ts. (3) detectCircularDependencies + dfsDetectCycle are duplicated in both analyzer.ts and graph-algorithms.ts (both now fixed to void return, but code is identical). Consolidate to single source — either parameterize the global version to accept position info, or import from one canonical location.

title: UX: Passing directory path crashes with raw Bun error
description: Running 'stepdown-rule analyze fixtures/' crashes with 'error: Directories cannot be read like files' from Bun internals. The glob library treats 'fixtures/' as a literal path. Should detect directory paths and auto-append **/*.ts or show a helpful message.

title: Fix nested fixer putting functions before logic
description: fixer.ts:393-394 reorderBlockStatements puts reorderedStmts before otherStatements. This is backwards — the nested rule says logic should come BEFORE nested function declarations. Swap to [...otherStatements, ...reorderedStmts].

title: UX: Fix errors say 'No changes' instead of 'Failed'
description: In cli.ts:250, when a fix fails with errors, the message says 'No changes: file.ts'. This is misleading — it should say 'Failed: file.ts' or 'Error: file.ts'. 'No changes' implies success (nothing needed fixing).

title: Extract named FunctionNode type, delete info: null
description: The type { node: ts.Node; info: null } appears 6+ times across fixer.ts, ast-node-visitors.ts, and ast-graph-builder.ts. The info field is never read, never carries data — it is a phantom from a prior design. All three experts flagged this (3/3 consensus). Extract a named type FunctionNode = { node: ts.Node } in a shared location (e.g. ast-node-visitors.ts alongside CategorizedNodes). Delete the info: null field entirely.

title: Remove dead FunctionInfo.dependencies field
description: analyzer.ts:298,392 — FunctionInfo.dependencies is always set to [] and never populated. Actual dependency data lives in the callGraph Map. Either remove the field from FunctionInfo type or populate it properly and document the contract.
close_reason: Already resolved: FunctionInfo no longer has a dependencies field

title: Extract nested violation detection from analyzer.ts
description: analyzer.ts is 661 lines with 30+ functions serving two distinct responsibilities: stepdown violation detection and nested violation detection. The nested detection logic spans lines 63-72, 192-425, and 625-660 (~250 lines). Extract to a dedicated nested-violation-detector.ts module. Also move the 6 file-local interfaces (NestedViolationContext, StatementProcessContext, FunctionDeclParams, VariableDeclParams, ViolationCheckParams, VariableStatementContext) from the bottom of analyzer.ts to co-locate with their consumers. This addresses both SRP and the interfaces-at-bottom readability issue.

title: Fixer6: Fix/analyze loop non-idempotent
description: Running stepdown-rule then --fix repeatedly does not converge. In ff-elysia: 22 violations → fix → 27 violations → fix → 22. Violations oscillate between runs instead of reaching 0. Root cause: fixer reordering creates new violations (Fixer3) while resolving others; topo sort does not minimize total violations.

title: Fixer2: Dependency graph incompleteness - partial reordering
description:
Root cause: The fixer's dependency graph (buildDependencyGraph in fixer.ts) does not capture all function relationships. When building the graph at lines 204-221, it may miss functions that are defined after they're called or not properly link all callers to their callees.

Evidence: The fixer reports 'reordered N functions' but violations still remain. For example, cart-composition.ts shows 'reordered 0 functions' yet violations persist where composeCartDomain (line 47) calls sessionStoreToRepository (line 22).

The dependency extraction (extractDependenciesFor at line 229) may not be finding all call sites, especially for complex call patterns in factory functions that create objects with methods.
close_reason: Verified: extractDependenciesFor finds deps in returned object method bodies (ts.forEachChild traverses). Added regression test.

title: Refactor: Naming and analyzer micro-refactors
description: analyzer: Rename violations_for_function to violationsForFunction (camelCase). filterOutCircularViolations: build functionsInCycles with new Set(circularDependencies.flat()). findFunctionNode: remove or shorten 'simplified implementation' comment per comment policy. fixer extractFunctionName: use node.name.getText(sourceFile) where type is already narrowed (remove redundant ?.).
acceptance_criteria: camelCase naming; flat() for cycle set; comment removed/shortened; optional chaining fixed; bun test and typecheck pass
notes: Description typo fix: first word should be 'analyzer' (no leading space).
close_reason: violationsForFunction; flat(); comment trim; optional chaining

title: Refactor: FixResult duplication - createUnfixedResult helper
description: In fixer.ts, introduce createUnfixedResult(file: string, errors: string[]): FixResult returning { file, fixed: false, originalContent: '', fixedContent: '', reordered: 0, errors }. Use it in createNoViolationsResult, createCircularDependencyResult, and the fixFileWithErrorHandling catch block. Single place for unfixed result shape.
acceptance_criteria: createUnfixedResult used in all three call sites; FixResult shape unchanged; bun test and typecheck pass

title: Fixe1: Arrow functions in const declarations not properly reordered
description:
Root cause: The fixer does not properly track arrow functions assigned to const variables in its dependency graph. When building the dependency graph in fixer.ts, arrow functions like 'const mapValidOrders = (...) => ...' are categorized but the topological sort doesn't handle them correctly. When functions like validateAndParseOrder are moved to fix violations for one caller, it creates new violations for arrow functions that call it.

Example: src/infrastructure/surreal/order-repository.ts has mapValidOrders (line 118) calling validateAndParseOrder (line 106). The fixer reordered some functions but left this violation. Similarly in wishlist-repository.ts with parseSingleWishlist calling validateWishlist.

Impact: Arrow functions in const declarations are not being reordered correctly, leaving violations unfixed.
close_reason: Fixed: populate functionNames before extracting deps; factory+arrow consts now order correctly

title: Refactor: Clean code and architecture review (epic)
description: Epic for refactors from clean code/architecture review: shared AST utils, FixResult helper, naming and micro-refactors, config loader sync, optional return type normalization. Goal: concision, expressiveness, maintainability; no functional or performance regressions.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads (vyi, 1vw, 1r9, 5aw, 46z) completed; vet passes
close_reason: All 5 refactor beads done; vet pass

title: Refactor: Normalize checkVariableDeclaration return type (optional)
description: analyzer checkVariableDeclaration currently returns string | null | undefined. Normalize to string | null (treat 'not a variable statement' as null) so callers do not handle undefined, or document the three-way contract. Low priority; do after core refactor beads.
acceptance_criteria: Return type string | null or documented; call sites updated; bun test and typecheck pass

title: Refactor: Config loader - getFullConfigJsonSchema sync + static import
description: In config/loader.ts: static import ConfigSchema from ./schema; change getFullConfigJsonSchema from async to sync, return ConfigSchema.toJsonSchema(). Removes dynamic import and simplifies API. Note: breaking if any caller awaits it.
acceptance_criteria: getFullConfigJsonSchema is sync; static import from ./schema; callers updated if any; bun test and typecheck pass

title: Fix idempotency issue in ff-elysia tool
description: Ran analysis tool in ff-elysia project and found it's not idempotent. See ff-elysia-output.md for details.
close_reason: Added ff-elysia integration test; converges when ff-elysia available (skips if not)

title: Implement StepdownRule (ViolationRule): analyze + fix
description: Extract stepdown detection and fix into StepdownRule implementing ViolationRule. analyze: use ctx to return stepdown violations (filter circular). fix: reorder top-level functions using ctx.dependencyGraph (current fixer logic).
acceptance_criteria: StepdownRule in registry; analyze and fix match current behavior

title: Fixer3: Reordering creates new violations (global opt issue)
description:
Root cause: The topological sort in the fixer performs LOCAL optimization (fixing one function's dependencies) without considering GLOBAL impact. When it reorders validateAndParseOrder to fix violations from createSurrealOrderRepository calling it, it creates NEW violations because the arrow functions (mapValidOrders, parseSingleOrder) now call a function that appears above them.

The topological sort at lines 261-298 in fixer.ts doesn't use a true dependency-aware ordering that minimizes violations across ALL functions. It simply reverses a standard topological sort which assumes callees should come before callers - opposite of stepdown rule.

Example: In order-repository.ts - fixing createSurrealOrderRepository's violations by moving helper functions creates new violations with the arrow functions that call those same helpers.
close_reason: Stable topo sort: use source order as tiebreaker when DAG has multiple valid orderings. Reduces ping-pong; bead fixtures converge.

title: Refactor: build RuleContext once per file, run rule.analyze for enabled rules
description: Single parse per file; build RuleContext (functions, callGraph, dependencyGraph) once. Replace current analyze pipeline with: for each enabled rule, run rule.analyze(ctx); aggregate violations per rule. Keep backward-compat AnalysisResult shape or adapt callers.
acceptance_criteria: One parse per file; all enabled rules analyze same ctx; violations aggregated

title: Define ViolationRule interface and RuleContext type
description: Add ViolationRule interface: id, analyze(ctx) => Violation[], fix(ctx, violations) => string. Add RuleContext type: parsedFile, functions, callGraph, dependencyGraph. Place in src/ or types; no barrel. Keep existing StepdownViolation and NestedFunctionViolation as Violation union.
acceptance_criteria: ViolationRule and RuleContext defined; types used in one placeholder or test

title: Fixer: 22 persistent violations in ff-elysia - failure mode taxonomy
description: Master reference for violations remaining after stepdown-rule --fix. See .beads/22-violations-analysis.md. Failures map to: FM1 Nested in callbacks (session) -> db8; FM2 Arrow const ordering (order-repo, wishlist) -> 27g; FM3 Fix creates new violations (cart, container) -> 96h; FM4 Dep graph gap -> 1e0; FM5 Mutual pairs (product-handlers) -> 96h. New beads: 0om idempotency, aka top-level-only, hje analyzer-fixer divergence.

title: Fixer7: Fixer only processes sourceFile direct children
description: Functions nested inside VariableStatement initializers (e.g. inside .derive() callback) never enter the fixer pipeline. session.ts: getSessionId, ensureSessionCookie inside sessionPlugin = new Elysia().derive(()=>{...}). categorizeNodes uses ts.forEachChild(sourceFile) - nested blocks not traversed for function extraction. Root cause of Fixer5 (db8).
close_reason: Completed: transformNestedBlocks reorders functions inside ArrowFunction/FunctionExpression/FunctionDeclaration bodies (e.g. .derive() callbacks)

title: Fixer5: Nested/inline functions inside .derive() blocks not detected
description:
Root cause: The analyzer's extractFunctions (analyzer.ts lines 52-79) only looks for top-level function declarations and variable statements. Functions defined inside method chains like .derive(), .on(), .decorate(), or other Elysia plugin methods are nested inside these call expressions and not captured as top-level functions.

Example: In src/plugins/session.ts line 39, 'ensureSessionCookie' is defined inside the .derive() chain. The analyzer doesn't see it as a top-level function that can be reordered. The violation 'ensureSessionCookie calls getSessionId which appears above it' persists because the fixer cannot reorder inline functions.

The findContainingFunction at line 734 correctly identifies these as nested, but there's no mechanism to extract and reorder them.
close_reason: Verified: analyzer extracts nested functions from callbacks; fixer (aka) reorders them. Tests added.

title: Deprecate legacy analyzeFiles/fixFiles or adapt to pipeline facade
description: Either expose new runPipeline(patterns, config, enabledRules) and keep analyzeFiles/fixFiles as thin facades that call it with all rules, or deprecate and move callers to pipeline. Preserve index/cli public API.
acceptance_criteria: CLI and programmatic API work; no breaking change or documented migration

title: Fixer8: Analyzer and fixer use separate code paths
description: Fixer does not consume analyzer output. fixFiles calls analyzeFiles but only uses result.file and violation/circular counts - not the actual violations or call graph. fixParsedFile rebuilds deps via extractDependenciesFor. Mismatch: analyzer finds violations fixer never addresses; fixer may extract different deps. Evidence: same file fixed but different violations on next analysis.

title: Epic: Rule pipeline refactor (parse once, ViolationRule, registry, selective execution)
description: Refactor to: parse AST once per file; shared RuleContext; ViolationRule interface (analyze + fix); rule registry; CLI --rules for selective execution; run all enabled analyses then each enabled fix in sequence. Aligns with docs/rule-pipeline-architecture.md.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads done; parse once per file; analyze all enabled rules on shared context; fix each enabled rule in order; --rules stepdown,nested works; vet passes
close_reason: All child beads completed; parse once, ViolationRule, registry, --rules, runPipeline facade; vet passes

title: Refactor: run rule.fix per enabled rule in sequence (content chained)
description: Fix phase: for each enabled rule that has fix and has violations, run rule.fix(ctx, violations); set content = result before next rule. Order from registry. Write file once after all fixes.
acceptance_criteria: Fixes run in registry order; content passed between fixes; single write

title: Add rule registry and CLI --rules for selective execution
description: Implement RuleRegistry: register(ViolationRule), getEnabled(ids?: string[]), list(). Add CLI option --rules stepdown,nested (default all). Config or options pass enabled rule ids to analyzer/fixer.
acceptance_criteria: Registry holds rules; CLI --rules filters; default runs all

title: Fixer4: Non-exported vs exported variable functions handled inconsistently
description:
Root cause: In analyzer.ts line 66, there's different handling for exported vs non-exported variable statements: 'if (ts.isVariableStatement(node) && !hasExportModifier(node))'. This means exported arrow functions are handled differently from non-exported ones.

The fixer likely has similar logic (though less visible) that treats exported functions differently. This can lead to inconsistent behavior where some violations are detected but not fixed, or different tracking for exported vs non-exported functions.

Evidence: The pattern appears in multiple files where the fixer reports 0 functions reordered but violations still exist (e.g., cart-composition.ts, container.ts). The analyzer may correctly identify the violation but the fixer may not recognize these functions as needing reordering due to export handling differences.
close_reason: Completed: removed hasExportModifier filter in extractFunctions, set isExported correctly in createVariableFunctionInfo

title: Add project rule: each analysis must have a fix implementation
close_reason: AGENTS rule + violation-coverage module + regression test

title: Wire registry into pipeline; update violation-coverage and tests
description: Orchestrator uses registry: resolve files, per file parse once -> build ctx -> run enabled rule.analyze -> run enabled rule.fix in order -> write. Update violation-coverage to iterate registry; ensure uj1 and analyzer-fixer tests pass with new pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline uses registry; existing tests pass; uj1 still enforces fix coverage per rule

title: Implement NestedRule (ViolationRule): analyze + fix
description: Extract nested-function detection and fix into NestedRule implementing ViolationRule. analyze: use ctx to return nested violations. fix: reorder nested block statements (current transformNestedBlocks logic).
acceptance_criteria: NestedRule in registry; analyze and fix match current behavior

title: Extract reusable logic into unified modules
close_reason: Unified reusable modules: ast-graph-builder.ts, graph-algorithms.ts, ast-node-visitors.ts. All tests pass with functional parity. Integrated with fixer.ts and analyzer.ts.

title: Refactor: Add shared AST utils (isFunctionLike, getPosition)
description: Add src/ast-utils.ts with: (1) isFunctionLike(node) - arrow or function expression, single name used by analyzer and fixer; (2) getPosition(sourceFile, node) returning 1-based { line, column }. Wire analyzer to replace isArrowFunctionOrFunctionExpression and repeated getLineAndCharacterOfPosition boilerplate; wire fixer to replace isFunctionExpression. Preserve 1-based semantics and all call sites.
acceptance_criteria: ast-utils.ts exists; analyzer and fixer import and use it; bun test and typecheck pass; no behavior change
close_reason: ast-utils.ts added; analyzer+fixer wired; tests pass

title: Align legacy and rule-based fix pipelines
description: fixer.ts has two divergent fix paths: (1) Legacy: reorderFunctionDeclarations() does top-level + nested in one pass. (2) Rule-based: fixFileWithRules() calls each rule's fix() sequentially, using reorderTopLevelOnly() or applyNestedOnly() separately. These paths can produce different results for the same input. Align them so both produce identical output.
notes: Expert review (2/3 consensus): Add characterization/equivalence tests that run representative fixtures through BOTH paths and assert bit-identical output. Pin current behavior before unifying. Key divergence: analyzeParsedFile calls filterOutCircularViolations explicitly while analyzeWithRules delegates via violationsToAnalysisResult. Fix paths differ: fixFileWithRules iterates rules with re-parse; processAnalysisResult does single-pass reorder+nested.

title: Fix hasNoExternalVariableReferences false negatives
description: analyzer.ts:340-368 — canConvertToFunctionDeclaration check is wrong. hasNoExternalVariableReferences returns false for ANY identifier not in functionNames, including function parameters, imports, and type references. A function like 'const add = (a, b) => a + b' falsely reports canBeFunctionDeclaration: false because a and b aren't in functionNames. Need to exclude parameters, imports, and type refs from the check.
